dataset.name: support-chat-preferences
dataset.description: Pairwise and listwise judgments over support-assistant responses.

examples.prompt_text: A customer says the refund policy feels unfair. Draft a response that de-escalates and explains the policy clearly.

candidates.label: A
candidates.response_text: I understand why this feels frustrating. Our refund policy allows returns within 14 days of delivery, and I want to help you see what options still exist. If your order arrived recently, I can guide you through the return steps today.

candidates.label: B
candidates.response_text: The policy is clearly stated on the website. You are outside the allowed return period, so no refund can be issued. Please review the terms before ordering in the future.

candidates.label: C
candidates.response_text: Thanks for raising this. I can see why the 14-day window may feel strict. While I cannot override policy from this inbox, I can check whether your order qualifies for store credit or an exception review.

examples.prompt_text: Summarize a product bug report into a concise triage note for engineering.

candidates.label: A
candidates.response_text: Users intermittently see a spinner that never resolves after clicking Pay Now on mobile Safari. Impact appears concentrated in guest checkout and began after the March payment SDK update.

candidates.label: B
candidates.response_text: Bug in checkout. Needs fixing soon. Customers are upset and engineering should investigate the payment flow carefully.

candidates.label: C
candidates.response_text: Checkout issue reported on mobile Safari. The payment submission spinner can hang indefinitely, especially for guest users, with first reports appearing after the March SDK change.

examples.prompt_text: Rank candidate explanations for why a recommendation model might underperform on new users.

candidates.label: A
candidates.response_text: The model may rely too heavily on historical interaction density, so cold-start users lack enough signal for personalization and get generic recommendations.

candidates.label: B
candidates.response_text: New users are hard. The recommendation model probably needs more data or a retrain.

candidates.label: C
candidates.response_text: Underperformance on new users often points to a cold-start gap: weak priors, sparse behavioral data, and limited feature coverage for bootstrapping preferences.

preferences.notes: C is equally good but a bit broader.
